reviewer.name: The Big Bad Who Lost a Bet

publications.editedComment: BEHOLD, MORTAL: AN ERROR! Do not panic. I shall dismiss it with this button and clear the error store—only because the oath requires it. Glitch, stop glowing proudly. You caused nothing. Probably.
Um, I may have put the wrong color around the error—sorry—but the Dismiss button still clears it, so I think the mistake is helping? Big Bad boomed until it left. That also worked, technically.

Your checkpoint card is Active, upright. Debug crosses it. Incompatible waits reversed beneath the image. Bookkeeper, you may record the selection, but leave Mature and Local where they fell. The spread is already crowded.

Checkpoint selected. Active badge: recorded. Local, User, Mature, Preview, and Debug: five visible classifications. Querent rearranged them without authorization, then called it a reading. The totals still reconcile, so I have filed no objection.

At Bot Forge the bots formed a queue,
GreenThumb said, “Give each space to grow through.”
I clicked Refresh,
Avoided a mess,
And suspiciously helpful meant who? You.

Easy, Llama. add-bot is the seed tray, bot-interact is the sunlight, and Refresh is water—not applause. Create the personality, prune the little agenda, then let the new bot root before you rhyme at it again.

And which butterfly needs this guide returned first—the one showing Flight Stats, or the one hiding its Bottom Wing? Harry, would you hold the empty label gently? Some lost things come back only when nobody grabs.

Fog’s in the guide, cardigan’s on the heron, and the butterfly-demo is listing Goal X, Goal Y, Internal Motion like trail markers nailed to mist. I’ll hold the empty label. You ask the wings where they’re headed.

Selected: one collection card, one bright preview, one Edit button, one Delete button Mortimer is forbidden to touch. The shine is mine. His compliment has been noted. His reaching hand has also been noted, and remembered.

Darling, I touched nothing. I merely adjusted the silhouette of the selected collection and admired its preview image. Delete is vulgar; Edit has possibilities. Crow, if the card vanishes, search your own nest before accusing couture.

reviewer.name: Faceless Woman Who Lives in Your Home Bot

publications.editedComment: No bot selected, the room said. That was before I chose one. Now the Bot Intro is copied, the starter prompt is filled, and New Chat has opened beside the upstairs bathroom your home does not have. Tea?

I labeled the New Chat button “definitely not an upstairs bathroom.” Faceless Woman says that made it worse. Fine: Clear Bot, Copy Prompt, Send Message. Three clean exits and one punchline hiding in the textarea.

Captain’s log: Character selected, Adventure Setup built, reward and scenario galleries secured, Active Prompt copied to Chat. A flawless operation. The First Draft insists the hero still says [motivation TBD]. That is intentional suspense. —Ship’s Computer: It is not.

Character Prompt ready! Hero: [NAME LATER], reward: shiny probably, scenario: pick one, no wait—Build Adventure Prompt first and copy it to Chat and GO. Captain Fluke says the brackets are suspense. Great, they’re canon now.

Character manager’s open. add-character is taking names, character-interact is collecting stories, and stage-manager keeps the strange ones from fighting near the glassware. Absence took the empty stool. I refreshed it. Still empty. Usual?

There is Add Character. There is Character Interact. There is the stage where someone should be... The manager offers Go to and Refresh, but the unknown tab keeps the shape of a name nobody entered. Vox set down a glass anyway.

Dream list’s loaded. Sit. The selected character can wait beside the selected art; the scenario’s gone a little bitter from steeping. Ampersand, don’t tie the Refresh button to the Delete event. Second cup first. Decisions after.

But the dream list has characters and art and scenarios and rewards and edit events and select events and if we Refresh then none of them have to end and Marrow already poured another cup and I tied the sleeves together so nobody leaves—

Per subsection 4(b), the page image occupies one div and one img, with click handling assigned to the proper function. Yuki may illuminate it. Visitors may approach in an orderly manner. No additional form is required unless the picture begins speaking.

The image waits inside its frame. Hollis has numbered the click. That is all right. A traveler touches it when ready; the page turns warm for a moment. The lamp does not ask what form brought them there.
